title: Chimichurri Flank Steak
description: Grilled flank steak topped with bright, herbaceous chimichurri sauce. A simple Argentine-inspired dinner that delivers bold flavor.
instructions: Season flank steak generously with salt and pepper on both sides.
Finely chop parsley, oregano, and garlic. Combine in a bowl with red wine vinegar, red pepper flakes, and olive oil. Stir well and set chimichurri aside.
Preheat grill or cast-iron skillet to high heat.
Grill steak 5-6 minutes per side for medium-rare.
Let steak rest 5 minutes, then slice thinly against the grain.
Spoon chimichurri generously over sliced steak and serve.

title: Thai Basil Chicken (Pad Krapow)
description: A quick Thai street food classic with ground chicken, fresh basil, and a savory-spicy sauce served over cauliflower rice.
instructions: Heat coconut oil in a large skillet or wok over high heat.
Add garlic and Thai chilies, stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add ground chicken, breaking it apart. Cook 5-6 minutes until browned.
Add diced bell pepper, coconut aminos, and fish sauce. Stir-fry 3-4 minutes.
Remove from heat and fold in Thai basil leaves until wilted.
Serve over cauliflower rice.

title: Lamb Kofta Bowls with Tahini Drizzle
description: Spiced ground lamb kofta served over a bed of roasted vegetables and greens with a creamy tahini dressing.
instructions: Combine ground lamb with cumin, coriander, cinnamon, garlic, parsley, and salt. Mix well and form into small oval kofta shapes.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook kofta 3-4 minutes per side until browned and cooked through.
Whisk tahini with lemon juice and 2-3 tablespoons water to make a pourable dressing.
Assemble bowls with mixed greens, cucumber, cherry tomatoes, and kofta.
Drizzle tahini sauce over each bowl and serve.

title: Chicken Verde Sweet Potato Casserole
description: Shredded chicken smothered in salsa verde, layered with sweet potato rounds and roasted to perfection.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375F.
Saute onion and garlic in avocado oil until soft, about 4 minutes.
Mix shredded chicken with salsa verde and cumin.
Layer sweet potato rounds on the bottom of a 9x13 baking dish. Season with salt.
Spread chicken mixture over sweet potatoes. Add another layer of sweet potatoes on top.
Cover with foil and bake 30 minutes. Remove foil and bake 10 more minutes until sweet potatoes are tender.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve.

title: Pan-Seared Salmon with Mango Salsa
description: Crispy-skinned salmon fillets paired with a bright tropical mango salsa for a light and vibrant dinner.
instructions: Combine diced mango, red onion, jalapeno, cilantro, and lime juice in a bowl. Season with a pinch of salt and set aside.
Pat salmon fillets dry and season with salt, pepper, and smoked paprika.
Heat avocado oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Place salmon skin-side down and cook 4 minutes until skin is crispy. Flip and cook 3-4 more minutes.
Serve salmon topped with mango salsa.

title: Beef and Broccoli Stir-Fry
description: Classic Chinese takeout made at home with a savory coconut aminos sauce, tender beef, and crisp broccoli.
instructions: Heat avocado oil in a large wok or skillet over high heat.
Add beef slices in a single layer and sear 2 minutes per side. Remove and set aside.
Add broccoli to the wok with 2 tablespoons water. Cover and steam 3 minutes until bright green and crisp-tender.
Add garlic and ginger, stir-fry 30 seconds.
Pour in coconut aminos and arrowroot slurry. Stir until sauce thickens, about 1 minute.
Return beef to the wok, toss everything together with sesame oil.
Garnish with green onions and sesame seeds.

title: Turkey-Stuffed Zucchini Boats
description: Zucchini halves loaded with seasoned ground turkey, tomatoes, and herbs, baked until golden and tender.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400F.
Scoop out zucchini centers with a spoon, creating boats. Chop the scooped flesh and reserve.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Saute onion and garlic 3 minutes.
Add ground turkey, breaking apart. Cook 5-6 minutes until browned.
Add chopped zucchini flesh, diced tomatoes, Italian seasoning, and salt. Cook 3 minutes.
Fill zucchini boats with turkey mixture and place on a baking sheet.
Bake 20 minutes until zucchini is tender. Garnish with fresh basil.

title: Coconut-Crusted Fish Tacos
description: Flaky white fish with a crispy coconut crust served in lettuce wraps with avocado crema and pickled onions.
instructions: Mix shredded coconut, almond flour, salt, and garlic powder in a shallow dish.
Dip cod strips in beaten egg, then press into coconut mixture to coat.
Heat coconut oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Pan-fry fish 3-4 minutes per side until golden and cooked through.
Mash avocado with lime juice and a pinch of salt for crema.
Assemble tacos in lettuce leaves with fish, shredded cabbage, and avocado crema.

title: Dairy-Free Butter Chicken
description: A rich and creamy butter chicken using coconut cream instead of dairy, with aromatic Indian spices and tender chicken thighs.
instructions: Heat ghee in a large pot over medium-high heat. Season chicken with salt and turmeric, then sear 3-4 minutes until browned. Remove and set aside.
In the same pot, saute onion until softened, about 4 minutes. Add garlic and ginger, cook 1 minute.
Stir in tomato paste, garam masala, and cumin. Cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Add crushed tomatoes and coconut cream. Stir to combine and bring to a simmer.
Return chicken to the pot. Simmer 20-25 minutes until sauce thickens and chicken is cooked through.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve over cauliflower rice.

title: Korean Beef Lettuce Cups
description: Sweet and savory Korean-style ground beef served in crisp lettuce cups with quick-pickled carrots and sesame.
instructions: Toss julienned carrots with rice vinegar and a pinch of salt. Set aside to pickle.
Heat sesame oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add ground beef, garlic, and ginger. Cook 6-8 minutes, breaking up meat, until browned.
Pour in coconut aminos and red pepper flakes. Stir and cook 2 more minutes.
Spoon beef into lettuce cups. Top with pickled carrots, green onions, and sesame seeds.

title: Harissa Roasted Chicken Thighs
description: Bone-in chicken thighs rubbed with spicy harissa paste and roasted on a sheet pan with sweet potatoes and red onion.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425F.
Toss sweet potatoes and red onion with 2 tablespoons olive oil, salt, and pepper. Spread on a sheet pan.
Rub chicken thighs with harissa paste and remaining olive oil. Nestle among the vegetables.
Roast 35-40 minutes until chicken skin is crispy and internal temp reaches 175F.
Garnish with parsley and serve.

title: Turkey Taco Skillet
description: A one-pan taco night with seasoned ground turkey, black beans, corn, and all the fixings served over rice or greens.
instructions: Heat avocado oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add ground turkey and cook 5-6 minutes until browned, breaking apart.
Add chili powder, cumin, smoked paprika, garlic powder, and salt. Stir to coat.
Add fire-roasted tomatoes, black beans, and corn. Stir and simmer 10 minutes.
Serve topped with sliced avocado and lime wedges.

title: Herb-Crusted Cod with Roasted Asparagus
description: Flaky cod baked with a fresh herb and almond crust, served alongside crispy roasted asparagus.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425F.
Toss asparagus with 1 tablespoon olive oil, salt, and pepper. Spread on a sheet pan.
Mix almond flour, dill, parsley, lemon zest, garlic powder, and 1 tablespoon olive oil.
Place cod fillets on the sheet pan. Brush with remaining olive oil and press herb mixture on top.
Bake 15-18 minutes until cod flakes easily and asparagus is tender.
Serve with lemon wedges.

title: Chicken Shawarma Bowls
description: Warmly spiced chicken shawarma served in bowls with turmeric cauliflower rice, crisp veggies, and tahini sauce.
instructions: Toss chicken with cumin, turmeric, smoked paprika, cinnamon, salt, and 1 tablespoon olive oil.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook chicken 4-5 minutes per side until charred and cooked through. Slice.
Saute cauliflower rice with remaining olive oil and a pinch of turmeric for 4 minutes.
Whisk tahini with lemon juice and 2-3 tablespoons water for dressing.
Assemble bowls with cauliflower rice, sliced chicken, cucumber, tomatoes, and red onion.
Drizzle with tahini dressing.

title: Slow Cooker Pot Roast
description: A hearty, fall-apart tender chuck roast with root vegetables, cooked low and slow with rich bone broth.
instructions: Season chuck roast generously with salt and pepper.
Heat avocado oil in a large skillet over high heat. Sear roast 3 minutes per side until deeply browned.
Place carrots, celery, onion, and garlic in the slow cooker.
Set seared roast on top of vegetables. Add tomato paste, bone broth, rosemary, and thyme.
Cover and cook on low for 8 hours or high for 5 hours until fork-tender.
Shred or slice the roast and serve with vegetables and cooking juices.

title: Mediterranean Turkey Burgers
description: Juicy turkey burgers packed with sun-dried tomatoes, olives, and herbs, served in lettuce wraps with lemon aioli.
instructions: Mix ground turkey with sun-dried tomatoes, olives, oregano, garlic, egg, and salt. Form into 4 patties.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Cook burgers 5-6 minutes per side until cooked through.
Stir together mayo and lemon juice for lemon aioli.
Serve burgers in lettuce wraps topped with lemon aioli.

title: Curried Cauliflower Soup with Chicken
description: A velvety, warming cauliflower soup with Indian curry spices and shredded chicken for a satisfying bowl.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Saute onion 4 minutes until soft.
Add garlic and curry powder, stir 1 minute until fragrant.
Add cauliflower florets and chicken broth. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer 15 minutes until cauliflower is tender.
Use an immersion blender to puree until smooth. Stir in coconut milk.
Add shredded chicken and heat through, about 3 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper. Serve hot.

title: Lemon Garlic Lamb Shoulder Chops
description: Tender lamb shoulder chops marinated in lemon, garlic, and rosemary then pan-seared to a beautiful crust.
instructions: Combine lemon juice, lemon zest, garlic, rosemary, 2 tablespoons olive oil, salt, and pepper in a bowl.
Coat lamb chops in the marinade. Let sit at least 15 minutes (or up to 4 hours in fridge).
Heat remaining olive oil in a cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear lamb chops 5-7 minutes per side for medium doneness.
Let rest 5 minutes before serving.

title: Sheet Pan Chicken Fajitas
description: Colorful peppers and onions with seasoned chicken thighs roasted on one pan for the easiest fajita night.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425F.
Toss chicken, peppers, and onion with avocado oil, chili powder, cumin, smoked paprika, garlic powder, and salt.
Spread in a single layer on a large sheet pan.
Roast 22-25 minutes, stirring halfway, until chicken is cooked and peppers are charred.
Serve with lime wedges and sliced avocado.

title: Miso-Glazed Salmon
description: Rich salmon fillets broiled with a sweet and savory white miso glaze, served with steamed bok choy.
instructions: Whisk together miso paste, coconut aminos, rice vinegar, sesame oil, and ginger.
Place salmon on a foil-lined baking sheet. Brush generously with miso glaze.
Let marinate 15 minutes while oven preheats to broil.
Broil salmon 8-10 minutes until glaze caramelizes and fish flakes.
Meanwhile, saute bok choy in avocado oil over medium-high heat for 3-4 minutes.
Serve salmon over bok choy, garnished with green onions and sesame seeds.

title: Beef Bolognese over Spaghetti Squash
description: A rich, slow-simmered meat sauce loaded with vegetables, served over roasted spaghetti squash strands.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400F. Brush spaghetti squash halves with 1 tablespoon olive oil, season with salt, and place cut-side down on a sheet pan. Roast 40 minutes.
Meanwhile, heat remaining olive oil in a large pot. Saute onion, carrots, and celery 5 minutes.
Add garlic and cook 1 minute. Add ground beef and brown 5-6 minutes, breaking apart.
Stir in crushed tomatoes, Italian seasoning, and salt. Simmer 25-30 minutes until thickened.
Scrape spaghetti squash strands with a fork. Serve bolognese over squash, garnished with basil.

title: Thai Coconut Shrimp Soup (Tom Yum Inspired)
description: A fragrant and spicy Thai-inspired soup with shrimp, mushrooms, and lemongrass in a coconut broth.
instructions: Bring chicken broth to a boil in a pot. Add lemongrass and ginger slices. Simmer 5 minutes to infuse.
Add mushrooms and cook 3 minutes.
Pour in coconut milk, fish sauce, and Thai chili. Return to a simmer.
Add shrimp and cook 3-4 minutes until pink and curled.
Remove lemongrass pieces. Stir in lime juice.
Serve in bowls garnished with cilantro.

title: Dairy-Free Chicken Piccata
description: Thin chicken cutlets dredged in almond flour, pan-fried, and finished with a bright lemon-caper sauce.
instructions: Season chicken cutlets with salt and pepper. Dredge in almond flour, shaking off excess.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook chicken 3 minutes per side until golden. Remove and set aside.
In the same skillet, add garlic and cook 30 seconds. Pour in chicken broth and lemon juice, scraping up browned bits.
Add capers and simmer 3 minutes until sauce reduces slightly.
Return chicken to the pan and spoon sauce over. Garnish with parsley.

title: Chipotle Beef Burrito Bowls
description: Smoky chipotle-seasoned beef over cilantro lime cauliflower rice with all the burrito fixings, no tortilla needed.
instructions: Toss sliced steak with minced chipotle, adobo sauce, cumin, and salt.
Heat 1 tablespoon avocado oil in a skillet over high heat. Sear steak 2-3 minutes per side. Set aside.
In the same skillet, saute cauliflower rice with remaining oil 4 minutes. Stir in lime juice and half the cilantro.
Assemble bowls with cauliflower rice, steak, shredded lettuce, cherry tomatoes, and avocado.
Garnish with remaining cilantro.

title: Egg Roll in a Bowl
description: All the flavors of a classic egg roll deconstructed into a quick stir-fry bowl with ground turkey and cabbage.
instructions: Heat avocado oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add ground turkey and cook 5-6 minutes until browned. Add garlic and ginger, stir 30 seconds.
Add coleslaw mix and stir-fry 4-5 minutes until cabbage is wilted but still has crunch.
Pour in coconut aminos, rice vinegar, and sesame oil. Toss to combine.
Top with green onions and sesame seeds.

title: Za'atar Grilled Chicken
description: Chicken breasts rubbed with fragrant za'atar spice blend, grilled and served with a bright Israeli-style salad.
instructions: Rub chicken breasts with za'atar, 2 tablespoons olive oil, and salt. Let sit 10 minutes.
Preheat grill to medium-high heat.
Grill chicken 6-7 minutes per side until internal temp reaches 165F.
Toss cucumber, tomatoes, red onion, and parsley with lemon juice and remaining olive oil.
Serve grilled chicken alongside the Israeli salad.

title: Tuscan Sausage and Kale Soup
description: A dairy-free take on zuppa toscana with Italian sausage, potatoes, kale, and a silky coconut milk broth.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Crumble sausage into pot and cook 5-6 minutes until browned. Remove and set aside.
In the same pot, saute onion 3 minutes. Add garlic and red pepper flakes, cook 1 minute.
Add chicken broth and potatoes. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer 12-15 minutes until potatoes are tender.
Stir in kale and coconut milk. Cook 3-4 minutes until kale is wilted.
Return sausage to the pot. Season with salt and pepper. Serve hot.

title: Blackened Chicken Avocado Salad
description: Cajun-spiced blackened chicken over a loaded salad with avocado, corn, black beans, and zesty lime dressing.
instructions: Mix smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, cayenne, and salt. Coat chicken evenly.
Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a cast-iron skillet over high heat. Cook chicken 5-6 minutes per side until blackened and cooked through. Let rest, then slice.
Whisk remaining olive oil with lime juice for dressing.
Assemble salad with romaine, avocado, corn, and tomatoes. Top with sliced chicken.
Drizzle lime dressing over the top.

title: Lamb Meatballs in Marinara
description: Tender spiced lamb meatballs baked and simmered in a homemade marinara sauce, served over zucchini noodles.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400F.
Mix ground lamb with almond flour, egg, half the garlic, oregano, cumin, and 1 teaspoon salt. Roll into 20 meatballs.
Place meatballs on a parchment-lined sheet pan. Bake 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, heat olive oil in a large pot. Saute onion and remaining garlic 3 minutes. Add crushed tomatoes and remaining salt. Simmer 10 minutes.
Add baked meatballs to the marinara. Simmer 5 more minutes.
Serve over raw or lightly sauteed zucchini noodles. Garnish with basil.

title: West African Chicken Peanut Stew
description: A rich, warming stew with chicken thighs, sweet potatoes, and a creamy peanut-tomato base inspired by West African cuisine.
instructions: Heat coconut oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Season chicken with salt and sear 3-4 minutes until browned. Remove.
Saute onion 3 minutes. Add garlic and ginger, cook 1 minute.
Stir in crushed tomatoes, peanut butter, chicken broth, and cayenne. Whisk until smooth.
Add sweet potato and chicken. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer 25-30 minutes until sweet potato is tender.
Serve garnished with cilantro and crushed peanuts.

title: Cilantro Lime Turkey Patties with Sweet Potato Fries
description: Bright, herby turkey patties with a crispy baked sweet potato fry side. A family-friendly weeknight winner.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425F.
Toss sweet potato fries with 2 tablespoons avocado oil, smoked paprika, and 0.5 teaspoon salt. Spread on a sheet pan and bake 25-30 minutes, flipping halfway.
Mix ground turkey with cilantro, lime zest, lime juice, garlic, remaining salt, and pepper. Form into 8 patties.
Heat remaining avocado oil in a skillet over medium heat. Cook patties 4-5 minutes per side until cooked through.
Serve patties alongside sweet potato fries.

title: Sesame-Crusted Ahi Tuna Steaks
description: Restaurant-quality seared ahi tuna with a sesame crust, served with a ginger-coconut aminos dipping sauce.
instructions: Whisk coconut aminos, ginger, rice vinegar, and sesame oil for dipping sauce. Set aside.
Pat tuna steaks dry and season with salt. Press sesame seeds onto all sides.
Heat avocado oil in a skillet over high heat until just smoking.
Sear tuna 1-1.5 minutes per side for rare (longer for medium).
Slice against the grain and serve with dipping sauce and green onions.

title: Enchilada Chicken Bake
description: A comforting layered casserole with shredded chicken, enchilada sauce, and plantain rounds standing in for tortillas.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375F.
Toss plantain slices with 1 tablespoon avocado oil and bake on a sheet pan 10 minutes to soften.
Mix shredded chicken with cumin, chili powder, diced onion, and salt.
Layer half the plantain slices in a 9x13 baking dish. Spread half the enchilada sauce, then all the chicken mixture, olives, remaining sauce, and remaining plantains on top.
Cover with foil and bake 25 minutes.
Top with sliced avocado and cilantro before serving.

title: Greek Chicken Skewers with Cucumber Dill Sauce
description: Marinated chicken skewers grilled to perfection, served with a creamy dairy-free cucumber dill dipping sauce.
instructions: Marinate chicken cubes in lemon juice, oregano, garlic, 2 tablespoons olive oil, and salt for at least 15 minutes.
Thread chicken, bell pepper, and onion onto skewers.
Mix grated cucumber, coconut cream, dill, remaining olive oil, and a pinch of salt for the sauce.
Grill skewers over medium-high heat 5-6 minutes per side until chicken is cooked through.
Serve skewers with cucumber dill sauce for dipping.

title: Roasted Beet and Herb-Crusted Lamb Chops
description: Inspired by Alain Passard's legendary beet preparations at L'Arpège. Slow-roasted beets in olive oil paired with herb-crusted lamb chops for an elegant, vegetable-forward dinner.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Toss beet wedges with 2 tbsp olive oil, garlic, thyme, salt, and pepper on a sheet pan.
Roast beets for 40-45 minutes, turning once, until tender and caramelized at the edges.
While beets roast, rub lamb chops with remaining olive oil, rosemary, salt, and pepper. Let come to room temperature.
Heat a cast-iron skillet over high heat. Sear lamb chops 3-4 minutes per side for medium-rare.
Rest lamb 5 minutes. Drizzle roasted beets with sherry vinegar and toss gently.
Arrange beets on plates, lean lamb chops against them, and scatter with fresh parsley.

title: Celery Root Purée with Roasted Chicken and Truffle Oil
description: A Passard-inspired celebration of celery root, transformed into a silky purée beneath perfectly roasted chicken thighs with a drizzle of truffle oil.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Pat chicken thighs dry, season generously with salt and pepper.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium-high. Sear chicken skin-side down 5 minutes until golden. Flip, add thyme sprigs, transfer to oven.
Roast chicken 25-30 minutes until internal temp reaches 165°F.
While chicken roasts, simmer celery root in salted water until fork-tender, about 20 minutes. Drain well.
Blend celery root with remaining olive oil, coconut cream, garlic, lemon juice, and salt until silky smooth.
Spoon celery root purée onto plates, nestle chicken thighs on top, drizzle with truffle oil, and finish with chives.

title: Passard-Style Stuffed Tomatoes with Lamb and Herbs
description: Inspired by Passard's famous stuffed vegetables. Ripe tomatoes filled with seasoned lamb, fresh herbs, and rice, slow-roasted until deeply caramelized.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Cut tops off tomatoes and scoop out pulp, reserving it. Season insides with a pinch of salt.
Sauté onion in 1 tbsp olive oil until soft, 4 minutes. Add garlic and cumin, cook 1 minute.
Combine lamb, rice, sautéed onion mixture, mint, parsley, salt, pepper, and 2 tbsp of reserved tomato pulp.
Fill each tomato generously with lamb mixture, mounding slightly. Place in an oiled baking dish.
Drizzle remaining olive oil over stuffed tomatoes. Pour remaining tomato pulp into the bottom of the dish.
Roast 45-50 minutes until tomatoes are blistered and filling is cooked through.
Scatter pine nuts over top and serve with pan juices spooned around.

title: Glazed Turnips with Seared Salmon and Chive Oil
description: Alain Passard elevates humble turnips to fine-dining status. Here, olive-oil-glazed baby turnips accompany crispy-skinned salmon with a vibrant chive oil.
instructions: Make chive oil: blend chives with 3 tbsp olive oil and a pinch of salt until smooth. Strain through a fine sieve and set aside.
Place turnips in a wide pan with broth, honey, 1 tbsp olive oil, and a pinch of salt. Bring to a simmer.
Cook turnips covered for 15 minutes, then uncover and let liquid reduce until turnips are glazed and golden, about 10 more minutes.
Season salmon with salt and white pepper. Heat remaining olive oil in a skillet over medium-high.
Sear salmon skin-side down 4 minutes until crisp. Flip and cook 2-3 minutes more for medium.
Plate glazed turnips, set salmon alongside, drizzle chive oil over everything, and finish with lemon juice.

title: Slow-Roasted Carrots with Harissa Chicken
description: Passard's reverence for the carrot meets North African spice. Whole carrots are slow-roasted until candy-sweet, served alongside harissa-rubbed roasted chicken.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Mix harissa, 1 tbsp olive oil, cumin, and half the salt. Rub all over chicken pieces.
Toss carrots with remaining olive oil, honey, garlic, and remaining salt on a large sheet pan.
Nestle chicken pieces among the carrots, skin-side up.
Roast 45-50 minutes until chicken skin is crispy and carrots are deeply caramelized and tender.
Squeeze lemon juice over the pan. Scatter cilantro over everything and serve directly from the pan.

title: Delicata Squash Gratin with Baked Eggs
description: An homage to Passard's iconic arpège egg. Roasted delicata squash rings cradling baked eggs in a pool of herb-infused olive oil — simple, stunning, and satisfying.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Toss squash rings with 2 tbsp olive oil, sage, garlic, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Arrange squash in a single layer on a sheet pan. Roast 20 minutes until tender and golden.
Arrange roasted squash rings in a baking dish, creating small wells between them. Crack eggs into the wells.
Season eggs with a pinch of salt and drizzle remaining olive oil over top.
Bake 12-15 minutes until egg whites are set but yolks remain slightly runny.
Serve over arugula dressed with a drizzle of balsamic vinegar.

title: Raw Vegetable Carpaccio with Pan-Roasted Cod
description: Passard's signature vegetable carpaccio reimagined: paper-thin shaved vegetables dressed in citrus vinaigrette alongside flaky pan-roasted cod.
instructions: Arrange shaved radish, beet, fennel, and cucumber in overlapping layers across four plates. Refrigerate while preparing fish.
Whisk together 2 tbsp olive oil, lemon juice, orange juice, and a pinch of salt for the carpaccio dressing.
Pat cod fillets dry and season with salt and pepper on both sides.
Heat remaining olive oil in a non-stick skillet over medium-high. Cook cod 4 minutes per side until golden and flaky.
Dress the vegetable carpaccio with citrus vinaigrette. Place cod alongside.
Finish with fresh dill, fennel fronds, and flaky sea salt.

title: Caramelized Onion Tarte Tatin with Seared Duck Breast
description: Passard's vegetable tarte tatin technique adapted GF/DF: onions caramelized in olive oil on a cassava-flour crust, paired with perfectly seared duck breast.
instructions: Make pastry: combine cassava flour and 0.5 tsp salt. Cut in coconut oil until crumbly. Add cold water, form into a disc, and refrigerate 20 minutes.
Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a 10-inch oven-safe skillet over medium-low. Place onion halves cut-side down. Cook 20 minutes, undisturbed, until deeply golden.
Drizzle balsamic and honey over onions, scatter thyme. Roll pastry to fit skillet and lay over onions, tucking edges.
Bake at 400°F for 20-25 minutes until crust is golden. Invert onto a plate.
Score duck skin in crosshatch. Season with salt and pepper. Place skin-side down in a cold skillet, cook over medium 12 minutes to render fat. Flip, cook 4 minutes for medium-rare. Rest 5 minutes.
Slice duck breast and fan alongside a wedge of onion tarte tatin.

title: Spring Vegetable Garden Broth with Poached Chicken
description: Passard's garden-to-table philosophy in a bowl. A crystal-clear broth brimming with spring vegetables and tender poached chicken — pure, clean, and restorative.

ingredients.name: leek
ingredients.notes: white and light green parts, thinly sliced

instructions: Bring bone broth to a gentle simmer. Season chicken with salt and white pepper, then slide into the broth.
Poach chicken at a bare simmer (never boil) for 18-20 minutes until cooked through. Remove and let rest.
Add carrots and leek to the broth. Simmer 5 minutes.
Add asparagus and snap peas. Simmer 3-4 minutes until vegetables are vibrant and just tender.
Slice poached chicken and divide among shallow bowls. Ladle vegetable broth over and around the chicken.
Finish each bowl with tarragon, chervil, lemon zest, and a drizzle of your best olive oil.

title: Whole-Roasted Cauliflower Steak with Herb Pistou and Lamb Sausage
description: Passard proved a vegetable could be the centerpiece. Thick cauliflower steaks roasted until golden, served with a bright herb pistou and crumbled lamb sausage for protein.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Brush cauliflower steaks with 2 tbsp olive oil, season with salt and pepper.
Roast cauliflower on a sheet pan for 25-30 minutes, flipping once, until deep golden and tender.
While cauliflower roasts, brown crumbled lamb sausage in a skillet over medium heat, 8-10 minutes. Drain and set aside.
Make pistou: blend basil, parsley, garlic, remaining olive oil, lemon juice, red pepper flakes, and a pinch of salt until a loose paste forms.
Plate cauliflower steaks, spoon lamb sausage around and over them.
Drizzle pistou generously, scatter pine nuts, and serve immediately.

title: Bone Marrow Roasted Root Vegetables with Herb Salad
description: Inspired by Dan Barber's nose-to-tail philosophy, roasted bone marrow bastes a medley of root vegetables for extraordinary depth of flavor. A celebration of the whole animal.
instructions: Preheat oven to 450°F. Place marrow bones cut-side up on a sheet pan and roast for 15 minutes until marrow is soft and beginning to bubble.
Scoop out about half the marrow and toss with carrots, parsnips, and turnips. Season with salt and pepper.
Spread vegetables on a sheet pan and roast at 425°F for 25-30 minutes, tossing halfway, until caramelized and tender.
Make herb salad: toss parsley, shallot, and capers with olive oil and lemon juice.
Serve roasted vegetables with remaining marrow bones alongside. Top with herb salad and flaky salt.

title: Whole Roasted Chicken with Pan Dripping Vegetables
description: Dan Barber's reverence for the whole animal meets simplicity — a perfectly roasted chicken whose drippings transform humble root vegetables into something extraordinary.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Rub chicken with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper. Stuff cavity with lemon halves, garlic, and 2 thyme sprigs.
Toss sweet potatoes, celery root, and onions with remaining olive oil, salt, and thyme. Spread in a roasting pan.
Set chicken directly on top of the vegetables, breast-side up.
Roast for 70-75 minutes until internal temp reaches 165°F and juices run clear. The vegetables will absorb all the drippings.
Rest chicken 10 minutes before carving. Serve over the caramelized vegetables, spooning any pan juices over top.

title: Grilled Steak with Carrot Top Pesto
description: Inspired by Barber's wastED philosophy — carrot tops that would be discarded become a vibrant, herbaceous pesto for a perfectly grilled steak.
instructions: Make pesto: blend carrot tops, basil, garlic, pine nuts, nutritional yeast, lemon juice, and olive oil until smooth. Season with salt.
Let steaks come to room temperature for 30 minutes. Pat dry and season generously with salt and pepper.
Heat grill or cast iron to very high heat. Brush steak with avocado oil.
Grill 4-5 minutes per side for medium-rare (130°F internal). Rest 8 minutes.
Slice against the grain and serve topped with generous spoonfuls of carrot top pesto.

title: Beet Green Wrapped Salmon with Roasted Beets
description: Root-to-leaf cooking at its finest — beet greens wrap salmon fillets while the beets roast alongside. Inspired by Barber's whole-vegetable philosophy.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Toss beets and shallots with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper. Roast for 25 minutes.
Blanch beet greens in boiling water for 30 seconds, shock in ice water, and pat dry.
Season salmon with salt, pepper, lemon zest, and dill. Wrap each fillet in 3 overlapping beet green leaves.
After beets have roasted 25 minutes, nestle wrapped salmon among the beets. Drizzle with olive oil.
Roast another 12-15 minutes until salmon is cooked through (145°F).
Drizzle plated fish and beets with remaining olive oil and red wine vinegar.

title: Braised Short Ribs with Caramelized Turnips
description: Barber champions overlooked vegetables — here, humble turnips are elevated through long braising with beef short ribs until both are impossibly tender.
instructions: Preheat oven to 325°F. Season short ribs generously with salt and pepper.
Heat avocado oil in a Dutch oven over high heat. Sear short ribs on all sides until deeply browned, about 3 minutes per side. Remove and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium. Sauté onion, carrots, and celery for 5 minutes. Add garlic and tomato paste, cook 1 minute.
Add vinegar to deglaze, scraping up browned bits. Add broth and rosemary. Return short ribs to pot.
Cover and braise in oven for 2 hours. Add turnips, cover, and continue braising 1 more hour until ribs are fall-off-the-bone tender.
Skim fat from surface. Serve ribs over turnips with braising liquid spooned over.

title: Chicken Liver Mousse with Seasonal Crudités
description: Offal is central to Barber's waste-nothing ethos. Silky chicken liver mousse made with coconut cream and olive oil, served with crisp farm vegetables.
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Sauté shallots until softened, 2 minutes. Add garlic and thyme, cook 30 seconds.
Add chicken livers, season with salt and pepper. Cook 3-4 minutes per side until browned outside but still slightly pink inside.
Transfer liver mixture to a food processor. Add coconut cream, remaining olive oil, and vinegar.
Blend until very smooth and silky, scraping down sides. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Transfer to a serving bowl, drizzle with olive oil, and serve with radishes, carrots, celery, and endive leaves for dipping.

title: Whole Roasted Cauliflower with Crispy Chicken Thighs
description: Barber's whole-vegetable approach meets practical family dinner. A whole cauliflower roasts until deeply caramelized alongside crispy chicken thighs.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Rub cauliflower all over with 2 tbsp olive oil, paprika, cumin, and half the salt.
Place cauliflower in center of a large roasting pan. Scatter garlic and lemon slices around it.
Season chicken thighs with remaining salt, pepper, and olive oil. Arrange skin-side up around the cauliflower.
Roast for 50-55 minutes until cauliflower is deeply browned and tender, and chicken skin is crispy (165°F internal).
Cut cauliflower into wedges and serve alongside chicken thighs. Spoon pan juices over everything and garnish with parsley.

title: Lamb Shoulder Braised over Root Vegetables
description: Barber's farm-to-table ethos in a single pot — an inexpensive lamb shoulder slow-braised until falling apart over a bed of seasonal roots that drink up the juices.
instructions: Preheat oven to 300°F. Season lamb shoulder generously with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven over high heat. Sear lamb on all sides until deeply browned, 8-10 minutes total. Remove.
Layer rutabaga, parsnips, carrots, onions, and garlic in the pot. Tuck in herb sprigs. Pour broth over.
Place lamb on top of vegetables. Cover tightly with lid.
Braise for 3-3.5 hours until lamb is completely tender and pulls apart with a fork.
Let rest 15 minutes. Pull lamb into large pieces, discarding bone. Serve over braised root vegetables with pan juices.

title: Grilled Fish Collar with Radish and Herb Salad
description: Fish collars are the most flavorful, most discarded cut — pure Barber waste-nothing philosophy. Grilled until crispy and served with a bright radish salad.
instructions: Season fish collars with salt, pepper, and coconut aminos. Let sit 10 minutes.
Heat grill or grill pan to high heat. Oil grates well.
Grill collars 5-6 minutes per side until skin is crispy and flesh is cooked through.
Meanwhile, toss radishes, herbs, scallions, lime juice, and olive oil together. Season with salt.
Serve grilled collars with radish herb salad piled on top.

title: Duck Leg Confit with Parsnip Purée
description: Confit in olive oil rather than duck fat — an adaptation of the classic that honors Barber's respect for slow technique and overlooked root vegetables like parsnips.
instructions: Season duck legs generously with salt, cover, and refrigerate overnight or at least 2 hours.
Preheat oven to 285°F. Rinse salt from duck legs and pat dry. Place in a snug oven-safe pot with olive oil, 4 garlic cloves, thyme, and bay leaves.
Cover and cook for 2.5 hours until meat is very tender and pulls easily from bone.
Meanwhile, simmer parsnips and remaining garlic in broth until very tender, about 20 minutes. Drain, reserving liquid. Blend with coconut cream until silky smooth, adding cooking liquid as needed.
Remove duck from oil. Heat a skillet over high heat and sear duck legs skin-side down for 3-4 minutes until skin is shatteringly crisp.
Serve duck over parsnip purée, garnished with chives.

title: Grilled Beef Heart Skewers with Chimichurri
description: Beef heart is lean, intensely beefy, and usually discarded — a perfect expression of Barber's nose-to-tail philosophy. Grilled hot and fast with bright chimichurri.

ingredients.name: beef heart
ingredients.notes: trimmed of fat and sinew, cut into 1.5-inch cubes

instructions: Make chimichurri: combine parsley, oregano, garlic, red onion, vinegar, olive oil, red pepper flakes, and a pinch of salt. Let sit at least 15 minutes.
Thread beef heart cubes onto skewers. Season with salt and pepper. Brush lightly with chimichurri.
Heat grill to very high heat. Grill skewers 2-3 minutes per side for medium-rare. Do not overcook — heart becomes tough.
Meanwhile, roast cubed sweet potatoes at 425°F for 25 minutes until tender and caramelized.
Serve skewers over sweet potatoes with extra chimichurri spooned over.

title: Celery Root Steaks with Roasted Turkey Breast
description: Thick-cut celery root steaks, seared and roasted until golden — Barber's approach to treating vegetables with the same reverence as meat, paired with juicy turkey.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Season turkey breast with salt, pepper, and minced garlic. Rub with 1 tbsp olive oil.
Roast turkey for 30-35 minutes until internal temp reaches 165°F. Rest 10 minutes before slicing.
Meanwhile, heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Sear celery root steaks 4 minutes per side until golden.
Add sage leaves to the pan, transfer to oven, and roast alongside turkey for 15-20 minutes until tender.
Whisk remaining olive oil with lemon juice and mustard for a simple vinaigrette.
Serve sliced turkey over celery root steaks on a bed of arugula, drizzled with vinaigrette.

title: Braised Oxtail with Sunchokes and Gremolata
description: Oxtail and sunchokes — two ingredients the mainstream overlooks. Barber-inspired slow braise transforms them into something extraordinary, finished with bright gremolata.
instructions: Preheat oven to 300°F. Season oxtails generously with salt and pepper.
Heat avocado oil in a Dutch oven over high heat. Sear oxtails in batches until deeply browned on all sides. Remove.
Sauté onion and carrots for 5 minutes. Add garlic and tomato paste, cook 1 minute.
Return oxtails, add broth and bay leaves. Bring to a simmer, cover, and braise in oven for 2.5 hours.
Add sunchokes, cover, and continue braising 45 minutes until sunchokes are tender.
Make gremolata: combine parsley, lemon zest, and 1 minced garlic clove.
Serve oxtails and sunchokes in shallow bowls with braising liquid. Top with gremolata.

title: Farm Egg Skillet with Wilted Garden Greens
description: Barber's seed-to-plate simplicity — farm eggs cooked in olive oil over a tangle of whatever greens are in season. Simple, nourishing, extraordinary when ingredients are good.

ingredients.name: mixed greens
ingredients.notes: kale, chard, beet greens, or spinach

instructions: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Brown ground turkey with salt and pepper, breaking into crumbles, 6-7 minutes. Remove and set aside.
In the same skillet, heat remaining olive oil. Add garlic and red pepper flakes, cook 30 seconds.
Add greens in batches, tossing until wilted, about 3 minutes. Add cherry tomatoes and cooked turkey.
Make 8 small wells in the greens. Crack an egg into each well. Season eggs with salt.
Cover and cook 4-5 minutes until egg whites are set but yolks are still runny.
Garnish with fresh herbs and serve straight from the skillet.

title: Roasted Cabbage Steaks with Lamb Chops
description: Thick cabbage steaks, charred and caramelized alongside lamb chops — Barber's technique of treating everyday vegetables as center-of-plate stars.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Brush cabbage steaks with 2 tbsp olive oil, season with salt and Aleppo pepper.
Place cabbage steaks on a sheet pan and roast for 15 minutes. Flip and roast 10 more minutes until deeply caramelized.
Meanwhile, season lamb chops with salt, pepper, garlic, and rosemary. Let sit 10 minutes.
Heat remaining olive oil in a cast iron skillet over high heat. Sear lamb chops 3-4 minutes per side for medium-rare.
Drizzle balsamic vinegar over cabbage steaks. Serve alongside lamb chops.

title: Turkey Meatballs in Fennel Broth with Fennel Fronds
description: The whole fennel — bulb, stalks, fronds — nothing wasted. Barber's root-to-leaf approach with turkey meatballs simmered in a fragrant fennel broth.

ingredients.name: fennel bulb
ingredients.notes: bulb sliced, stalks chopped, fronds reserved

instructions: Mix ground turkey with egg, almond flour, half the garlic, fennel seeds, salt, and pepper. Form into 16 meatballs.
Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Brown meatballs on all sides, about 6 minutes. Remove.
In the same pot, sauté onion and fennel stalks for 3 minutes. Add remaining garlic, cook 30 seconds.
Add broth and sliced fennel bulb. Bring to a simmer. Return meatballs to pot.
Simmer 20-25 minutes until meatballs are cooked through and fennel is tender.
Finish with lemon juice and remaining olive oil. Serve in bowls garnished with reserved fennel fronds.

title: Whole Roasted Trout Stuffed with Herb Garden
description: A whole fish, simply roasted and stuffed with herbs from the garden — Barber's farm-to-table at its most elemental and beautiful.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Halve fingerling potatoes, toss with 1 tbsp olive oil and salt, and spread on a sheet pan. Roast 15 minutes.
Score each trout 3 times on each side. Season inside and out with salt and pepper.
Stuff each cavity with herb sprigs, lemon slices, and garlic slices.
Push potatoes aside, place trout on the pan. Drizzle with remaining olive oil.
Roast 15-18 minutes until fish flakes easily and skin is crispy.
Serve whole trout over roasted potatoes with any pan juices.

title: Seared Beef with Smashed Cucumber and Herb Oil
description: Smashing cucumbers opens their cellular structure to absorb flavor — a technique Barber uses to transform simple produce. Paired with seared beef and verdant herb oil.
instructions: Make herb oil: blend mint, basil, garlic, and olive oil until smooth. Season with salt.
Smash cucumbers with the flat side of a knife, then tear into rough pieces. Toss with vinegar and a pinch of salt. Let marinate 10 minutes.
Pat steaks dry. Season generously with salt and pepper.
Heat avocado oil in cast iron over very high heat. Sear steaks 3-4 minutes per side for medium-rare. Rest 5 minutes.
Arrange smashed cucumbers on plates, top with sliced steak, drizzle herb oil over everything, and scatter with sesame seeds.

title: Waste-Nothing Chicken Soup with Root Vegetable Scraps
description: The ultimate Barber-inspired dish: a whole chicken yields meat for the soup while bones and vegetable scraps create the broth. Every part used, nothing wasted.
instructions: Place whole chicken in a large pot with vegetable peels, onion skins, herb stems, garlic, bay leaves, and vinegar. Cover with cold water by 2 inches.
Bring to a boil, reduce to a gentle simmer. Cook 60-70 minutes until chicken is cooked through.
Remove chicken. Strain broth through a fine mesh sieve, discarding scraps. Return broth to pot.
Chop carrots, celery, onions, and parsnips into bite-sized pieces. Add to broth and simmer 15-20 minutes until tender.
Meanwhile, shred chicken meat from the carcass, discarding skin and bones (or save bones for a second broth).
Return shredded chicken to soup. Season with salt and pepper. Serve topped with fresh parsley and celery leaves.

title: Roasted Delicata Squash Rings with Spiced Chicken
description: Delicata squash needs no peeling — the entire vegetable is edible. Barber's whole-vegetable spirit meets warmly spiced roasted chicken.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Mix cumin, coriander, cinnamon, salt, and pepper together.
Toss chicken drumsticks with 1 tbsp olive oil and half the spice mix.
Toss squash rings and red onion with remaining olive oil and spice mix.
Arrange chicken and squash on a large sheet pan in a single layer.
Roast for 30-35 minutes until chicken reaches 165°F and squash rings are caramelized and tender.
Whisk tahini with lemon juice and 2 tbsp water to make a drizzle. Serve chicken and squash drizzled with tahini sauce and pomegranate seeds.

title: Charred Whole Leeks with Pan-Seared Salmon
description: Leeks charred whole until sweet and smoky — Barber's celebration of the allium family in its simplest, most flavorful form, alongside crispy-skinned salmon.
instructions: Preheat oven to 450°F. Toss halved leeks with 2 tbsp olive oil and salt. Place cut-side down on a sheet pan.
Roast leeks for 20-25 minutes until deeply charred on the cut side and tender throughout.
Meanwhile, pat salmon dry and season with salt and pepper. Heat remaining olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear salmon skin-side down for 4 minutes until skin is crispy. Flip and cook 3 more minutes.
Add capers to the skillet in the last minute. Squeeze lemon over the salmon.
Serve salmon over charred leeks, spooning caper pan sauce over. Garnish with chives.

title: Grilled Chicken with Raw Kohlrabi Slaw
description: Kohlrabi — an underused, overlooked vegetable that Barber champions. Shaved raw into a crisp, apple-like slaw alongside simply grilled chicken.
instructions: Season chicken breasts with salt, pepper, and 1 tbsp olive oil. Let rest while you make the slaw.
Toss shaved kohlrabi, apple, mint, and cilantro together.
Whisk lime juice, remaining olive oil, and honey for the dressing. Toss with slaw and season with salt.
Grill chicken over medium-high heat, 5-6 minutes per side until cooked through (165°F). Rest 5 minutes.
Slice chicken and serve over kohlrabi slaw. Scatter with toasted pepitas.

title: Slow-Roasted Salmon with Beet Vinaigrette
description: Low and slow transforms salmon into silky custard — a technique Barber uses to coax the best from quality ingredients. Dressed with a vibrant beet vinaigrette.
instructions: Preheat oven to 275°F. Place salmon skin-side down on a parchment-lined sheet pan. Season with salt, pepper, and 1 tbsp olive oil.
Roast for 25-30 minutes until salmon is just opaque and incredibly silky (120°F internal for medium).
Meanwhile, blend roasted beet, shallot, vinegar, and remaining olive oil until smooth. Season with salt.
Arrange watercress on plates. Place slow-roasted salmon on top.
Drizzle beet vinaigrette over and around the salmon. Garnish with fresh dill.

title: Broccoli Stem Soup with Shredded Chicken
description: Broccoli stems are usually thrown away, but they make an incredibly silky soup — a perfect example of Barber's wastED concept. Topped with shredded rotisserie chicken.

ingredients.name: broccoli stems
ingredients.notes: peeled and chopped (save florets for another use)

instructions: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Sauté onion until soft, 5 minutes. Add garlic, cook 1 minute.
Add broccoli stems and broth. Bring to a boil, then simmer 15-18 minutes until stems are very tender.
Blend soup until silky smooth using an immersion blender. Stir in coconut cream.
Season with salt and pepper. Ladle into bowls.
Top each bowl with shredded chicken, a drizzle of olive oil, and toasted pumpkin seeds.

title: Lamb Burgers with Turnip Top Pesto
description: Turnip greens are typically discarded but make a peppery, nutrient-dense pesto — Barber's waste-nothing ethos applied to a satisfying lamb burger wrapped in lettuce.
instructions: Make turnip top pesto: blend turnip greens, basil, garlic, walnuts, nutritional yeast, lemon juice, and olive oil until smooth. Season with salt.
Mix ground lamb with cumin, salt, and pepper. Form into 4 thick patties.
Heat a grill or cast iron skillet over high heat. Cook patties 4-5 minutes per side for medium (160°F).
Grill red onion slices alongside the burgers until charred and softened.
Wrap each lamb patty in butter lettuce leaves with tomato, grilled onion, and a generous spoonful of turnip top pesto.
